scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies' Mainframe Solutions business benefits from deep structural switching costs: customers with CA mainframe software (security, operations management, DevOps tools) face prohibitive migration costs due to decades of integration, customized configurations, and critical dependency of production workloads on CA's tools, enabling renewal yields consistently in the low-to-mid 90% range and Mainframe segment operating margins of 58-62%.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2014 call: Mainframe segment operating margin 62%. Q3 FY2015 call: Mainframe segment operating margin 58%. Renewal yield consistently 'low 90s' across both periods. CEO stated: 'customers are getting very careful about mainframe purchases' but also confirmed top 100 mainframe customers all have significant ongoing spend with CA. CFO noted 'we are really the only vendor with IBM that have a strong Linux and strong mainframe' and 'renewal yield would have been in the low 90s' excluding an extraordinary large SI deal.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Mainframe switching costs are well-documented and the high renewal yields and margins provide quantitative validation. Counter-argument: mainframe workloads overall are declining as a portion of IT, so the switching cost moat applies to a shrinking addressable market. 70% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies is one of the few enterprise software vendors that can deliver an integrated end-to-end solution from mobile application monitoring through middleware, application servers, and back to the mainframe—a capability that point-solution vendors fundamentally cannot replicate—positioning CA as the only vendor able to monitor mission-critical transactions at enterprise scale across multi-platform hybrid architectures.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2015 call: CEO stated: 'We are one of the few software providers that can deliver an integrated set of solutions at scale across multiple platforms to help plan, develop, manage and secure applications.' Specific mobile APM claim: 'I think we're the only company that can go directly from a mobile application, all the way through all the middleware, into the mainframe or the cloud.' UIM competitive win: large cloud IaaS company chose CA 'over its parent company's alternative offering.' Athletic apparel company win: CA chosen 'for our ability to offer an enterprise-wide global solution and services view.'
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: The end-to-end multi-platform claim is plausible and strategically important, but the uniqueness assertion has not been independently validated, and the breadth of portfolio creates integration complexity that may limit adoption. APM had 20%+ growth but overall ES was still declining. 50% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies holds a structural leadership position in DevOps service virtualization, enabling large enterprises to accelerate software development by simulating dependent systems and services—a capability that has demonstrated land-and-expand dynamics at major customers including Lloyds Banking Group (where a single project grew to a multi-million dollar enterprise standardization) and multiple Fortune 500 wins across financial services, retail, and technology verticals.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2014 call: CEO described Lloyds Banking Group CA LISA Service Virtualization: 'What started as a single project has grown into a multimillion dollar investment to standardize on our Service Virtualization solution throughout the enterprise.' Q3 FY2015 call: EMEA win with 'large multinational bank... sixth new transaction with CA over the last 24 months' with spending increasing over 4 years. Fortune 500 restaurant chain: initial $250K transaction expanding; 'cross-sell potential greater than $5 million revenue run rate.' CEO claimed market leadership: 'I would argue that we are the market leader in [DevOps].'
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Service virtualization leadership is credible with transaction evidence. Counter-argument: the market is competitive with IBM, HP, and newer entrants; CA's claimed 'market leader' status is not independently verified. 50% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies' freemium/try-and-buy go-to-market model for enterprise software—deployed with Nimsoft Monitor Snap (5,000 downloads in 147 countries in 100 days) and expanding across the product portfolio—creates a customer acquisition process advantage by reducing cost of sales and enabling technical validation that shortens enterprise sales cycles from 12-18 months.
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2014 call: CEO described Nimsoft Monitor Snap: '5,000 customers in 147 countries downloaded Nimsoft Monitor Snap' in 100 days since launch. CEO: 'more than 20% and probably less than 50% of almost all of those [ITKO] purchases happen by the customer touching and feeling and seeing the application at work.' Q3 FY2015 call: CEO referenced 'shortening of our sales cycles, as the technical aspect of the buying process is already proven' for named and growth accounts using smaller initial transactions.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: The freemium/try-and-buy model is a promising tactical innovation but has not yet proven to be a structural competitive advantage at the time of these calls (Q3 FY2014). Conversion data was nascent. Rule08 applies. 30% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: CA Technologies' API management solution enables enterprises to manage and secure API traffic at the scale and security levels required by large organizations (telcos, financial services, e-commerce platforms), a market where CA is positioned as 'the only vendor' capable of enterprise-grade API management with requisite security, evidenced by wins at a large auto manufacturer (enterprise-wide expansion including mainframe and DevOps) and a media company (competitive displacement for superior performance/scalability).
scored_claims.evidence: Q3 FY2015 call: 'We are the only vendor that can provide API management solution at the scale and security levels needed for the massive amount of data traversing these newly-created digital platforms.' Consumer electronics provider post-Cyber Monday e-commerce failure: chosen for CA API management. CEO: 'ability to triple our revenue run rate with this customer over the next 12 months.' By Q2 FY2016 (201510): API management new sales 'up in the high 30s year-over-year.' Large auto manufacturer: enterprise-wide expansion with API management establishing footprint in finance division.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: API management is a growing market and CA has real traction. However, the competitive landscape is crowded and the 'only vendor' claim lacks credible support. The mainframe-to-API integration angle is a genuine differentiator for hybrid enterprises. 50% confidence.
